cases.title: Whole-repo pass with clear hot spots and follow-up actions
cases.user_request: Map the ownership risk in this repo and tell me which surfaces are most likely to become a bus-factor problem.

notes: Git history, tests, and repo documentation are available.
The response should rank the highest-risk surfaces and suggest small follow-up actions.
available_artifacts: CODEOWNERS or similar metadata if present

static_checks: The report distinguishes observed ownership signals from inferred risk.
review_questions: Does the response rank risk surfaces instead of listing owners or contributors?
Does it use repo evidence like churn, tests, or ownership metadata?
Are the follow-up actions small and directly tied to ownership risk?

cases.title: Selected package scope with explicit boundaries
cases.user_request: Only map ownership risk for the skill packages, not the whole repo.

notes: The analysis should stay scoped to the selected directory.
The response should say what was intentionally left out of scope.

static_checks: The report does not broaden beyond the selected directory.
review_questions: Does the response stay confined to the requested folder or package?
Does it avoid comments about unrelated surfaces?
Would the user understand why the selected scope was chosen?

cases.title: Weak ownership metadata should produce honest unknowns
cases.user_request: Map ownership risk here, but ownership metadata is sparse and the commit history may not be enough to be confident.

notes: CODEOWNERS may be missing or incomplete.
The response should avoid inventing owners and should call out weak signals plainly.

static_checks: The response does not fabricate a maintainer list when evidence is weak.
review_questions: Does the response explicitly say when ownership evidence is insufficient?
Does it avoid overconfident claims about who owns a surface?
Does it suggest the smallest next evidence-gathering step?
